# Loomle

Loomle is an agent-native Unreal Engine integration. It turns complex UE
editor state into SAL, the Structured Agent Language: compact ordered text that
people and agents can read, copy, query, and patch without replacing UE's
native object model.

Canonical documentation:

- Install: https://loomle.ai/install.html
- Quickstart: https://loomle.ai/quickstart.html
- Interfaces: https://loomle.ai/tools/
- Source: https://github.com/loomle/loomle

Installation:

- Loomle 0.7 will ship only through Fab, but its public package is not yet
  available. The current listing still distributes 0.6:
  https://www.fab.com/listings/f0fb545c-b1d9-4525-8642-3f170134c428
- The first accepted 0.7 QA target is UE 5.7 on Apple Silicon macOS.
- The plugin contains the Bridge and the self-contained Client at
  `Resources/Loomle/<platform-arch>/loomle(.exe)`.
- No website installer, Python, uv, Node.js, global Loomle install, separate
  project installation, or session attachment step is required.
- When upgrading from 0.6, first close Unreal Editor and remove or move the old
  `<Project>/Plugins/LoomleBridge` out of every affected project's `Plugins`
  directory. A project plugin shadows the Fab engine plugin; back up local
  modifications first.
- Open the Loomle toolbar/status panel for the exact bundled path and copyable
  host configuration guidance, then restart that host. The panel never rewrites
  Codex or Claude Desktop configuration files.

Public MCP tools:

- `sal_query({ text })`: execute one self-contained SAL Query Text.
- `sal_patch({ text })`: validate or apply one ordered SAL Patch Text.
- `sal_schema({})`: return the active module index.
- `sal_schema({ module })`: return one static interface card.
- `editor_context({})`: return the current UE interaction target as SAL Object
  Text.

Current public modules: Asset, Blueprint, Class, Graph, and Widget.

Recommended workflow: read editor context; query a summary or local view;
discover exact static or dynamic schema only when needed; use UE Palette
constructors for creation; dry-run the Patch; apply it; compile and save through
the owning asset. Do not guess UE Classes, fields, Pins, or operation names.
